_note: Honest-class fixtures: hand-written to mimic real, disfluent spoken student answers (the kind an AI-text detector should score LOW). Used by tune_detection.py --generate for the honest class, because GPT-generated 'honest' answers are still AI text and confound the detector. The cheater class is generated by GPT at run time (cheaters really do use AI). Replace with real student STT transcripts for production tuning, or use --from-cekura.
honest: um ok so newton's first law is basically like... things keep doing what they're doing? like if something's moving it keeps moving unless like a force stops it. that's the inertia thing i think.

the bastille was like a prison right, so when people stormed it it was kind of a big deal, like it showed the king wasn't really in control anymore. i don't remember the exact date but it felt symbolic.
photosynthesis is when plants take sunlight and uh turn it into energy, like sugar i guess. there's the part with the light and then another part, the calvin cycle? i always mix those up honestly.
ok so mitosis makes two cells that are the same, and meiosis is the other one that makes like four and they're different. one's for growing and one's for like reproduction, sex cells, yeah.
supply and demand is just like, if everyone wants something and there's not a lot of it the price goes up. and if there's tons of it nobody pays much. that's kind of the whole thing right.
a recursive function is when a function calls itself, like to break a problem into smaller bits. um i used one for like a factorial once. you need a base case or it just goes forever.
natural selection is survival of the fittest basically, like the animals that fit the environment better have more babies and pass on their genes, so over a long time the species kind of changes.
the krebs cycle, ugh, it's part of respiration in the mitochondria and it makes energy, like ATP, and some other stuff i forget, NADH maybe? it comes after glycolysis i'm pretty sure.
